summary: Add branch-convention guards to todo close / todo work
description:
Make `todo close` and `todo work` enforce branch conventions instead of
documenting them.

`todo close <id>`:
- Refuse to run unless current branch is `todo/<id>` (or `todo/<parent-id>`
  for child tickets). Error message must include the exact `git checkout` /
  `todo work` command to run instead.
- Refuse to run unless at least one commit on the branch (since base) has
  a message matching `todo:<id>` (catches the "did work but never used
  the prefix" failure mode).
- Both checks bypassable with `--force` for emergencies; print a loud
  warning when used.

`todo work <id>`:
- If --skip-branch is NOT set and current branch is already a non-matching
  `todo/<other-id>` with uncommitted changes, refuse with a clear message
  rather than silently checking out.

Done contract: tests in tests/ that cover (a) close on wrong branch fails
with right exit code + message, (b) close with no todo:<id> commits fails,
(c) --force overrides both, (d) work refuses to leave dirty working tree.

resolution.note: CLI guards added for close (branch + commit-prefix checks, --force bypass) and work (dirty-tree refusal). 16 new tests, full suite 127 passing.
